title: Tennis Star Carlos Alcaraz Out of French Open Due to Wrist Injury
summary: Bad news for tennis fans! Carlos Alcaraz, the 22-year-old tennis star from Spain, will miss the French Open next month. He hurt his right wrist while playing in Barcelona. Alcaraz has won the French Open two times in a row — in 2024 and 2025. He is the world number two player. After tests, doctors said he should rest and not play in Rome or Paris. Alcaraz posted on Instagram: "This is a difficult time for me, but I am sure we will come out of this stronger." He started this year by winning the Australian Open. That made him the youngest man ever to win all four big tennis tournaments (the Grand Slam). He has seven Grand Slam titles total. His biggest rival, Jannik Sinner from Italy, is now the world number one. Sinner said Alcaraz is "definitely the best player" on clay and wished him a fast recovery. Sinner has never won the French Open, so now he has a big chance. Alcaraz hopes to be back for Wimbledon in July. We all hope to see him playing again soon!
why_it_matters: This story reminds us that even the best athletes get injured and need to take care of their bodies to come back stronger.

background_read: The French Open is one of the most famous tennis tournaments in the world, held every year in Paris, France.
It is played on clay, which makes the ball bounce slower and harder to hit.
Carlos Alcaraz is a young tennis star from Spain who has already won seven Grand Slam tournaments.
Article_Structure: WHO: Carlos Alcaraz and his rival Jannik Sinner.
WHAT: Alcaraz withdraws from the French Open because of a right wrist injury.
WHERE: The injury happened in Barcelona, and the tournament is in Paris.
WHY: Doctors said he must rest to avoid making the injury worse.

perspectives.description: Alcaraz is being smart by resting instead of playing hurt and risking more damage.
Fans are sad they won't see him play at the French Open, especially since he won the last two years.
With Alcaraz out, other players like Sinner have a better chance to win the tournament.
